subject: Human Resources
title: Lifelong Learning Culture
description: A quick win: understand Lifelong Learning Culture.

hook.text: A 22-year-old graduates and thinks learning is over. A 52-year-old enrolls in an AI course. Who's more future-proof?

buildup.text: Lifelong learning means continuously developing skills throughout a career—not just through formal education but daily practice.

discovery.text: Google and Amazon offer thousands of internal courses, learning stipends, and dedicated learning time—investing in growth.

twist.text: Most employees say their company doesn't give them time to learn. Training budgets are often the first cut during downturns.

climax.text: Organizations protecting learning time—even during busy periods—build workforces that adapt faster and innovate more.

punchline.text: The day you stop learning is the day your career starts expiring.

quiz.question: What happens to training budgets during downturns?
options: They're often the first to be cut
